pr.title: Cleanup alignment through pl code and the sysdolphin section
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Large assembly-only cleanup to repair section alignment across player-related melee code and many sysdolphin/baselib assembly files. The main structural change split the large asm/melee/lb/code_80019230.s range at 0x80019880 into a new asm/melee/lb/code_80019880.s object, and obj_files.mk was updated accordingly. Many files gained explicit `.balign 8` immediately after `.section` declarations for rodata, data, bss, sdata, sbss, and sdata2, while some padding-like NULL words were removed where the corrected section alignment made them unnecessary. The PR body states that remaining troublesome alignment cases were intentionally split out for later.
what_changed: Added explicit `.balign 8` directives after many `.section` declarations, including `.rodata`, `.data`, `.bss`, `.sdata`, `.sbss`, and `.sdata2`, to stabilize emitted section starts.
Changed asm/melee/lb/code_80019230.s text range comment from `0x80019230 - 0x8001D2B8` to `0x80019230 - 0x8001987C`, showing that the object no longer owns the later code range.
Moved the function block starting at `func_80019880` out of asm/melee/lb/code_80019230.s into the new asm/melee/lb/code_80019880.s file.
Updated obj_files.mk with one added line, consistent with adding the new split-out assembly object to the build.
Removed some manual padding words such as `.4byte NULL` after aligned strings in sdata sections, as seen in camera.s and code_8000F9F8.s.

Touched 65 files overall, mostly with very small alignment edits in sysdolphin/baselib modules such as AXDriver, initialize, jobj, lobj, pobj, robj, sislib, synth, video, cobj, mobj, tev, wobj, and related baselib assembly files.
Reduced asm/melee/lb/lbdvd.s by 225 deleted lines with only 3 additions, implying a similar alignment/splitting or cleanup effect, though the provided excerpt does not show the exact lbdvd hunks.
smart_moves: Used explicit `.balign 8` at section boundaries instead of relying on implicit assembler behavior or scattered ad hoc padding, making alignment intent searchable and repeatable.
Removed fake-looking `.4byte NULL` padding once section alignment was corrected, avoiding data symbols that only exist to paper over layout drift.
Split a large assembly file at a clean address boundary (`0x80019880`) rather than continuing to fight alignment for an oversized object range.

Kept unresolved alignment problems out of this PR according to the body: `Couldn't get some others right, so I've split them out for later.` This limited the cleanup to cases the author could make consistent.
decomp_lessons: When an object has persistent rodata/sdata/bss drift, check whether each section begins with the alignment expected by the original object; adding `.balign 8` immediately after `.section` can be the minimal fix.
Do not preserve manual NULL padding blindly. If padding was only compensating for missing section alignment, replace it with the correct `.balign` and remove the artificial word.

Large assembly slices can hide object-boundary alignment problems. Splitting the slice into separate object files at a known function/address boundary may be safer than forcing one file to cover too much.
After splitting an assembly file, update both the old file's address-range comment and the build object list so future contributors can trace the new object boundary.
Alignment cleanup can affect many modules with one-line diffs; keep such PRs mechanical and narrowly scoped so changes are reviewable despite many files.
naming_conventions: Existing placeholder function naming was preserved, e.g. `func_80019880`, `func_80019894`, and address-based file names like `code_80019230.s` and `code_80019880.s`.
Label names remained address-based placeholders such as `lbl_803B73B8`, `lbl_803BCCA0`, `lbl_804D3938`, and `lbl_804D7E00`.
No new semantic names or struct/header naming conventions are evidenced in this PR slice.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Place `.balign 8` immediately after section declarations to make the first symbol in each section begin at the expected boundary.
For sdata strings followed by removed `.4byte NULL`, prefer section-level alignment over dummy data padding when matching layout.
Split an assembly object at a function boundary and adjust the `.section .text` address comment to match the shorter range.
Use a separate assembly file named after the new start address when splitting an address-based code file, as with `code_80019880.s`.
Treat `.sdata2` and small-data sections as alignment-sensitive; this PR repeatedly adds `.balign 8` to these sections.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or formal reviews were present in the provided PR context.
The only rationale text from the PR body was: `Dealt with alignment issues in these sections. Couldn't get some others right, so I've split them out for later.`
follow_up_queries: Which remaining alignment issues were split out for later after PR #294?
Find later PRs that decompile or rename functions in asm/melee/lb/code_80019880.s.
Search for `.4byte NULL` used as padding after `.asciz` in sdata sections and verify whether `.balign 8` is the correct replacement.
Check whether all sysdolphin/baselib assembly files now start rodata/data/bss/sdata/sbss/sdata2 sections with the expected alignment.
Compare obj_files.mk around PR #294 to confirm the object ordering after adding code_80019880.s.
